sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes

Requested: Phase 1 funding to take Bifrost from working testnet to launch readiness, including hardening, security audits, formal verification, ecosystem and partner readiness, stewardship, and economic foundations.

Deliverables: Audited Bitcoin-Cardano bridge running on Cardano mainnet under controlled access, Federated and SPO threshold custody modes, Hardened economic model, Stewardship structure, SPO and dApp partner pipeline, Open-source smart contracts, off-chain tooling, and SDK, Reporting on operations, milestones, treasury disbursements, and on-chain activity
Deadline/expiry: 9-month delivery period from July 2026 to March 2027

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Bifrost is designed as a permissionless Bitcoin-Cardano bridge secured by Cardano's SPO ecosystem that brings BTC onto Cardano as native fBTC.
Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests ₳12,332,031, including a 10% refundable contingency, for Phase 1 work by FluidTokens and Lantr Engineering.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Bifrost is already live on testnet under Catalyst Fund 14, with working peg-ins and peg-outs and participating SPOs.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): By the end of Phase 1, Bifrost will be an audited bridge running on Cardano mainnet in both federated and SPO threshold custody modes under controlled access.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposer states that custody of locked BTC is distributed across 400+ Cardano SPOs weighted by delegation, requiring a threshold representing a majority of delegated stake.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposer states Bifrost will have no bridge token, no founder allocation, and no built-in ongoing revenue claim for the founding teams.
missing: Independent evidence for: Bifrost is already live on testnet under Catalyst Fund 14, with working peg-ins and peg-outs and participating SPOs.
Independent evidence for: By the end of Phase 1, Bifrost will be an audited bridge running on Cardano mainnet in both federated and SPO threshold custody modes under controlled access.

Independent evidence for: The proposer states that custody of locked BTC is distributed across 400+ Cardano SPOs weighted by delegation, requiring a threshold representing a majority of delegated stake.
Independent evidence for: The proposer states Bifrost will have no bridge token, no founder allocation, and no built-in ongoing revenue claim for the founding teams.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "Bifrost is designed as a permissionless Bitcoin-Cardano bridge secured by Cardano's SPO ecosystem that brings BTC onto Cardano as native fBTC." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Bifrost is already live on testnet under Catalyst Fund 14, with working peg-ins and peg-outs and participating SPOs." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
